job_statement: When I need to implement continuous deployment for cloud-native applications, I want to use a declarative approach that manages infrastructure and application configurations through Git, so I can create repeatable processes across multi-cluster Kubernetes environments and automate complex deployments
related_jobs: Manage application lifecycle across multiple environments
desired_outcomes: Minimize time required to deploy applications across multiple clusters
Ensure configuration changes are auditable and versioned
Minimize deployment and release cycle time
evidence: understanding-openshift-gitops-reduced.adoc -> Section 'About GitOps', lines 7-19 [module: modules/about-gitops.adoc, type: CONCEPT]
notes: Source module: modules/about-gitops.adoc (CONCEPT). This is a foundational concept section explaining GitOps principles. The job focuses on understanding and adopting GitOps as a deployment methodology.

job_statement: When deploying applications to multiple clusters in different environments, I want to ensure consistency using a GitOps tool that maintains cluster resources through continuous monitoring and reconciliation, so I can keep configurations in sync with their desired state
desired_outcomes: Ensure clusters maintain consistent state across environments
Minimize time to detect configuration drift
Reduce manual intervention required for configuration sync
Ensure configuration changes can be automatically or manually resynced
evidence: understanding-openshift-gitops-reduced.adoc -> Section 'About Red Hat OpenShift GitOps', lines 21-36 [module: modules/about-redhat-openshift-gitops.adoc, type: CONCEPT]
notes: Source module: modules/about-redhat-openshift-gitops.adoc (CONCEPT). Explains how Red Hat OpenShift GitOps uses Argo CD to implement GitOps. Focus on execution and monitoring aspects.

job_statement: When managing multiple OpenShift clusters, I want to automate configuration management tasks including state synchronization, change application/reversion, and application promotion, so I can efficiently manage cluster configurations and application deployments at scale
parent_job: Ensure application consistency across multiple clusters
prerequisites: Configure Git repositories with desired state
desired_outcomes: Ensure all clusters have similar states for configuration, monitoring, and storage
Minimize time to apply or revert configuration changes across multiple clusters
Reduce errors when promoting applications from staging to production
Ensure templated configurations are correctly associated with environments
evidence: understanding-openshift-gitops-reduced.adoc -> Section 'Key features', lines 38-47 [module: modules/about-redhat-openshift-gitops.adoc, type: CONCEPT, subsection of main module]
notes: Source module: modules/about-redhat-openshift-gitops.adoc (CONCEPT). This is a subsection listing key automation tasks. Represents a user story showing how administrators use OpenShift GitOps features to accomplish the main job of ensuring cluster consistency.
